definition: A wholesome quality or wholesome dhamma relevant to ethical training and practice.
notes: The project keeps wholesome quality as the default because it is readable and preserves the repo's governed handling of kusala while avoiding over-narrowing dhamma to one English equivalent everywhere. Wholesome dhamma remains acceptable in source-facing prose. Wholesome thought is allowed only in explanatory contexts where mano is clearly engaging thought-content and a narrower practical gloss helps, such as simple explanations of gladdening citta in ānāpānasati-related instruction.

context_rules.context: In most ethical and doctrinal contexts
In source-facing prose where dhamma should remain audible
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when the broader dhamma range matters.

context_rules.context: In explanatory practice prose where mano is working with thought-content to gladden citta
context_rules.notes: Allowed explanatory narrowing, not the default doctrinal rendering.

version_notes: Expanded from a thin placeholder so practice prose can distinguish broad wholesome qualities from narrower explanatory wholesome thoughts.
